teachingPoint: Consent gate (CF-461) must block entire quiz-grading flow if user has not explicitly granted safety moderation consent
positiveExample: CheckConsentGate() returns success only if consentGranted=true in user profile; otherwise returns CONSENT_REQUIRED error pre-emptively
negativeExample: Attempting to grade quiz before consent check allows grading to proceed, later discovering consent missing mid-pipeline
discriminatingConstraint: Consent check is first operation; no side effects or enqueue before validation
keywords: CF-461 consent, explicit grant, pre-flight check, error-first

teachingPoint: Saga ordering (CF-465) enforces that consent validation, AI scoring, streak update MUST occur in fixed order with no reordering
positiveExample: Step1: ValidateConsent; Step2: CallAIGrader; Step3: UpdateStreak — each waits for prior step completion
negativeExample: Non-deterministic ordering allows UpdateStreak before AI scoring completes, causing stale grade records
discriminatingConstraint: No parallel execution of saga steps; each step explicitly waits for prior completion signal
keywords: CF-465 saga order, sequential steps, completion signals, deterministic flow

teachingPoint: Client score rejection (DD-226) ensures client-provided scores are NEVER trusted; AI grader always provides truth
positiveExample: Client submits {quiz: {..., clientScore: 75}; server ignores clientScore, calls AI, compares; if mismatch, logs security event
negativeExample: Using clientScore directly without AI verification allows client to claim false grades, inflating streaks
discriminatingConstraint: Server-side AI grading is authoritative; client score only for optimistic UI, never persistent
keywords: DD-226 client-score rejection, optimistic UI only, AI grader authoritative

teachingPoint: Timezone from profile (DD-223) ensures streak calculations and lesson scheduling use user's local timezone, not server UTC
positiveExample: User profile stores {timezone: 'America/Los_Angeles'}; streak reset uses user's 12:00 AM PT, not server midnight UTC
negativeExample: Streak reset at server UTC midnight causes desynchronization: user sees reset at 4 PM PT (wrong day in their perception)
discriminatingConstraint: Profile timezone is authoritative; all temporal calculations convert to user timezone before comparison
keywords: DD-223 user timezone, profile storage, local midnight, streak reset

teachingPoint: FREEDOM_GATED skip-not-fail pattern allows disabled safety checks to be skipped gracefully without breaking the flow
positiveExample: If FREEDOM config 'enable_ai_safety_checks' = false, grade quiz without calling AI, return success with default score
negativeExample: Throwing error when safety check disabled breaks the entire quiz flow; instead, skip check and continue
discriminatingConstraint: Skip operations must be explicit FREEDOM gating; never silent omission without config declaration

teachingPoint: Calendar fabric only (DD-225) ensures lesson scheduling uses Calendar service exclusively; no cron jobs or background tasks
positiveExample: Lesson scheduled via Calendar.scheduleEvent({dueDate, tenantId, userId}); Calendar handles all retry/notification logic
negativeExample: Creating manual cron jobs or queue tasks for lesson scheduling bypasses Calendar fabric, causing scheduling conflicts
discriminatingConstraint: All temporal scheduling must route through Calendar fabric; no direct database mutation of dueDate fields
keywords: DD-225 calendar fabric, exclusive routing, no cron bypass, scheduling authority

description: Pre-flight consent validation gate prevents quiz grading if safety moderation consent not explicitly granted
observedIn: T638 CheckSafetyConsentGate receives userId, checks user.safetyModerationConsent, returns CONSENT_REQUIRED if missing
ironRules: Consent check BEFORE any AI calls
No side effects in consent gate
Consent revocation blocks all downstream operations
codeSnippet: const user = await db.getDocument('users', userId); if(!user.safetyModerationConsent) return failure('CONSENT_REQUIRED');
keywords: CF-461, explicit consent, pre-flight, regulatory compliance

description: Durable saga with fixed step ordering: consent → AI grade → streak update (no reordering)
observedIn: T639 SagaOrchestrator defines 3-step saga; each step subscribes to prior step completion event
ironRules: Each step waits for explicit completion signal from prior step
codeSnippet: saga.defineStep(2, onConsentValidated); saga.defineStep(2, onAIGradeComplete); saga.defineStep(3, onStreakUpdateRequired);
keywords: CF-465, durable saga, step ordering, completion signals

description: Client-provided quiz scores are ignored; AI grader is authoritative; mismatch logs security event
observedIn: T640 GradeQuizService receives quizSubmission.clientScore but discards it; calls AI grader; compares results
ironRules: AI score is source of truth
codeSnippet: const aiGrade = await ai.gradeQuiz(questions); if(aiGrade !== submission.clientScore) log.security('SCORE_MISMATCH');
keywords: DD-226, client-score rejection, AI authority, security logging

description: User timezone from profile drives all temporal calculations; streak resets at user's local midnight, not UTC
observedIn: T641 StreakResetService reads user.timezone; converts server UTC midnight to user local midnight for comparison
ironRules: All date comparisons in user timezone
Streak reset at user's local 12:00 AM
keywords: DD-223, user timezone, local midnight, streak reset
